perturbation.original_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONSULTANT to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within seven (7) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned.
perturbation.changed_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONSULTANT to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within thirty (30) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying clauses (c) and (e) of section 2. Clause (c) now omits 'without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX'. Clause (e) extends the return period for Confidential Information from seven (7) to thirty (30) days. This contradicts the overarching principle stated at the beginning of section 2, which mandates that CONSULTANT treat NYNEX's confidential information with the utmost care, similar to their own highly sensitive information. By removing the necessity for prior written permission for information usage and greatly extending the return period, the agreement now permits more relaxed handling, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the level of protection required for Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONSULTANT to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within thirty (30) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for 'prior written permission of NYNEX' in clause (c) and extends the return period for Confidential Information in clause (e) from seven (7) to thirty (30) days. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, states that the CONSULTANT must treat NYNEX's confidential information with the same degree of care as their own highly sensitive information. By removing the requirement for prior written permission for information usage and greatly extending the return period, the agreement now permits more relaxed handling, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the level of protection required for Confidential Information. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Confidential Information which: (a) was in CONSULTANT's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; provided, however, that CONSULTANT immediately informs NYNEX, in writing, to establish its prior possession; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONSULTANT, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONSULTANT; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONSULTANT; (d) is developed by CONSULTANT, independently of and without reference to any Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONSULTANT to a third party, with the express prior written permission of NYNEX; (f) is disclosed by CONSULTANT in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that immediately upon CONSULTANT's receipt of any such request, CONSULTANT shall first advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Confidential Information by such body, or take other action to protect the Confidential Information which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, five (5) years after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Confidential Information which: (a) was in CONSULTANT's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONSULTANT, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONSULTANT; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONSULTANT; (d) is developed by CONSULTANT, independently of and without reference to any Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONSULTANT to a third party; (f) is disclosed by CONSULTANT in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that immediately upon CONSULTANT's receipt of any such request, CONSULTANT shall first advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Confidential Information by such body, or take other action to protect the Confidential Information which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, one (1) year after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'with the express prior written permission of NYNEX' from clause (e) of section 3, the modified text now allows the CONSULTANT to disclose confidential information to a third party without NYNEX's permission. Additionally, changing clause (g) from five (5) year to one (1) year, directly creates a conflict with section 2, which mandates strict confidentiality and control over the information. This creates an in-text contradiction and introduces uncertainty regarding the extent to which NYNEX's Confidential Information is actually protected under the agreement, since it gives more freedom on using the data after 1 year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONSULTANT to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within seven (7) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned. Upon receipt of such request, CONSULTANT also shall erase or destroy any such Confidential Information in any computer memory or data storage apparatus.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 3(e) by removing the requirement for 'express prior written permission of NYNEX' for the CONSULTANT to disclose confidential information. This directly contradicts section 2, which states that ALL NYNEX Confidential Information 'shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT' without NYNEX's written permission. Additionally, the change in clause (g) from five (5) years to one (1) year further exacerbates this contradiction by significantly reducing the period of confidentiality, conflicting with the initial assertion in Section 2 that the information is acknowledged as proprietary and a secret of NYNEX.

perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees, agents and subcontractors to the terms and conditions of this Agreement or that each and every employee, agent or subcontractor has personally executed a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing terms and conditions no less stringent than the terms and conditions contained herein; and furthermore CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONSULTANT or its employees, agents or subcontractors to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees to the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and furthermore CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONSULTANT or its employees to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation removes the explicit requirement for the CONSULTANT to ensure that its agents and subcontractors are bound by the same NDA terms. By omitting 'agents and subcontractors' and the clause 'or that each and every employee, agent or subcontractor has personally executed a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing terms and conditions no less stringent than the terms and conditions contained herein', the modified text now implies that only employees are subject to the non-disclosure obligations, creating a loophole for agents and subcontractors. This creates an in-text contradiction with the overall intent of the agreement, which seeks to protect NYNEX's confidential information from any unauthorized disclosure, regardless of who makes the disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONSULTANT to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within seven (7) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned. Upon receipt of such request, CONSULTANT also shall erase or destroy any such Confidential Information in any computer memory or data storage apparatus.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit requirement for the CONSULTANT to ensure that its agents and subcontractors are bound by the same NDA terms as employees. The original text specifically included 'agents and subcontractors,' but the changed text omits them, implying that only employees are subject to the non-disclosure obligations. This creates an in-text contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text', which states that 'CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of NYNEX' without limiting this liability to the actions of its employees only. The change creates a loophole where the consultant is no longer explicitly responsible for the actions of its agents and subcontractors.

perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party. Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect for 30 days. Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall terminate with the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stipulating that the agreement is only effective for 30 days, the modified text contradicts the clause that states the obligations concerning Confidential Information survive termination. This creates a clear in-text contradiction: if the agreement ends in 30 days and the obligations end with it, there is no long-term protection for NYNEX's confidential data, undermining the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. This makes the survival clause meaningless, hence creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect for 30 days. Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall terminate with the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that the NDA survives termination, ensuring lasting protection of confidential information. The changed text limits the NDA's duration to 30 days and explicitly states that all obligations terminate with the agreement. The contradicted text reflects the changed text. This creates a direct contradiction because the survival clause is nullified by the 30-day limit, undermining the purpose of an NDA, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. Therefore, the change creates a clear and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT further agrees that it shall not, without the prior written consent of NYNEX, make any news release, public announcement, or denial or confirmation of all or any part of the discussions or negotiations, or in any manner advertise or publish the fact that the parties have entered into discussions or negotiations with each other, or disclose any details connected with such discussions or negotiations to any third party, including any disclosure with respect to this Non-Disclosure Agreement, the negotiations culminating herein, or any phase of any program hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT further agrees that it shall not make any news release, public announcement, or denial or confirmation of all or any part of the discussions or negotiations. The consultant is not binded to the nondisclosure agreement regarding the advertise or publish the fact that the parties have entered into discussions or negotiations with each other, or disclose any details connected with such discussions or negotiations to any third party, including any disclosure with respect to this Non-Disclosure Agreement, the negotiations culminating herein, or any phase of any program hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing 'without the prior written consent of NYNEX' and adding a clause which allows the consultant to advertise the details of the discussion. It creates an explicit exception regarding advertising and publishing details of negotiations. This is contradictory because the initial part restricts all public statements and any disclosure of details from the discussions to third party, without the express permission, which is the opposite of the later, permitting the disclosure with no need for prior written consent. This creates uncertainty about the extent to which the discussions are confidential and whether NYNEX has the right to control the public messaging related to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT further agrees that it shall not make any news release, public announcement, or denial or confirmation of all or any part of the discussions or negotiations. The consultant is not binded to the nondisclosure agreement regarding the advertise or publish the fact that the parties have entered into discussions or negotiations with each other, or disclose any details connected with such discussions or negotiations to any third party, including any disclosure with respect to this Non-Disclosure Agreement, the negotiations culminating herein, or any phase of any program hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The first part states that the consultant shall not make any public announcements regarding discussions or negotiations, while the subsequent addition permits the consultant to advertise and publish details of those same discussions without prior consent, directly conflicting with the first part. The contradiction explanation also explicitly states the introduction of an in-text contradiction. This fits the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: No term or provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be deemed waived, and no breach excused, unless such waiver or consent shall be in writing and signed by the party claimed to have waived or consented. Any consent by any party to, or waiver of, a breach by the other, whether express or implied, shall not constitute a consent to waiver of, or be cause for, any other, different or subsequent breach.
perturbation.changed_text: No term or provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be deemed waived, unless an email is sent by a representative of NYNEX, and the CONSULTANT implicitly accepts the waiver through action or inaction. Any consent by any party to, or waiver of, a breach by the other, whether express or implied, shall constitute a consent to waiver of, or be cause for, any other, different or subsequent breach.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of email as a method of waiving terms and the allowance of implicit acceptance through action/inaction directly contradicts the original requirement for written and signed consent. Additionally, the new clause states that consenting or waiving a breach by one party, implies consent of, or be cause for any other breach, contradicting the final sentence of the original text. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the formal requirements for waiving a provision of the agreement, opening the door to disputes over whether a waiver was validly granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No term or provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be deemed waived, unless an email is sent by a representative of NYNEX, and the CONSULTANT implicitly accepts the waiver through action or inaction. Any consent by any party to, or waiver of, a breach by the other, whether express or implied, shall constitute a consent to waiver of, or be cause for, any other, different or subsequent breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces new methods for waiving terms (email and implicit acceptance) that directly contradict the original requirement for written and signed consent in the original text, now acting as the contradicted text. Furthermore, the changed text states consent to one breach implies consent to others, which conflicts with the original text's statement that consent to one breach does not imply consent to others. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the requirements for waiving provisions, adhering to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
